CREATE TABLE test_split (input String) ENGINE = Memory;
SELECT splitByChar(',', input) AS substrings, arrayJoin(substrings) AS substr FROM test_split;
SELECT count(*) FROM test_split WHERE length(splitByChar(',', input)) > 2;
SELECT input, splitByChar(',', input) FROM test_split WHERE input LIKE '%,%' ORDER BY input;
SELECT input, splitByChar(',', input, 2) AS substrings FROM test_split WHERE input LIKE '%,%' SETTINGS splitby_max_substrings_includes_remaining_string = 1;

CREATE TABLE table1 (name String) ENGINE = Memory; CREATE TABLE table2 (attributes String) ENGINE = Memory; INSERT INTO table1 VALUES ('John'), ('Jane'); INSERT INTO table2 VALUES ('age=30,height=5.5'), ('age=25,height=5.8'); SELECT t1.name, splitByChar(',', t2.attributes) AS attr_array FROM table1 AS t1 JOIN table2 AS t2 ON t1.name = 'John';
SELECT input, splitByChar(',', input, 6) FROM test_split;
